15	// newTestBackend returns a Backend rooted at a fresh temp dir. It exists so
16	// the chaos suite exercises the JSONL backend through the Backend interface
17	// (per ddx-bbdd7564 AC §5) rather than through *Store directly. Future
18	// backends conforming to Backend can be parameterized into the same tests by
19	// swapping this constructor.
20	func newTestBackend(t *testing.T) Backend {
21		t.Helper()
22		return newTestStore(t)
23	}

25	// TestChaos_ConcurrentAppendSafety spawns 10 goroutines each creating 10 beads
26	// against the same store. Verifies all 100 beads exist, no duplicates, no corruption.
27	func TestChaos_ConcurrentAppendSafety(t *testing.T) {
28		t.Parallel()
29		s := newTestBackend(t)
30	
31		const goroutines = 10
32		const beadsEach = 10
33		const total = goroutines * beadsEach
34	
35		var wg sync.WaitGroup
36		errCh := make(chan error, total)
37	
38		for g := 0; g < goroutines; g++ {
39			g := g
40			wg.Add(1)
41			go func() {
42				defer wg.Done()
43				for i := 0; i < beadsEach; i++ {
44					b := &Bead{Title: fmt.Sprintf("goroutine-%d-bead-%d", g, i)}
45					if err := s.Create(b); err != nil {
46						errCh <- fmt.Errorf("goroutine %d create %d: %w", g, i, err)
47					}
48				}
49			}()
50		}
51	
52		wg.Wait()
53		close(errCh)
54	
55		var errs []error
56		for err := range errCh {
57			errs = append(errs, err)
58		}
59		require.Empty(t, errs, "concurrent creates must not error: %v", errs)
60	
61		beads, err := s.ReadAll()
62		require.NoError(t, err)
63		assert.Len(t, beads, total, "all %d beads must be present", total)
64	
65		// Check for duplicates
66		seen := make(map[string]int)
67		for _, b := range beads {
68			seen[b.ID]++
69		}
70		for id, count := range seen {
71			assert.Equal(t, 1, count, "bead %s appears %d times (duplicate)", id, count)
72		}
73	
74		// Check no corruption: every bead must have a valid ID, title, and status
75		for _, b := range beads {
76			assert.NotEmpty(t, b.ID, "bead ID must not be empty")
77			assert.NotEmpty(t, b.Title, "bead title must not be empty")
78			assert.Contains(t, []string{StatusOpen, StatusInProgress, StatusClosed}, b.Status,
79				"bead %s has invalid status %q", b.ID, b.Status)
80		}
81	}

83	// TestChaos_AtomicStatusTransitions spawns 5 goroutines each cycling a single
84	// bead through open→in_progress→closed→open. After all iterations the bead
85	// must have a valid status and the JSONL file must be fully parseable.
86	func TestChaos_AtomicStatusTransitions(t *testing.T) {
87		t.Parallel()
88		s := newTestBackend(t)
89	
90		b := &Bead{Title: "status-churn"}
91		require.NoError(t, s.Create(b))
92		id := b.ID
93	
94		const goroutines = 5
95		const iterations = 20
96	
97		statuses := []string{StatusOpen, StatusInProgress, StatusClosed}
98		var wg sync.WaitGroup
99	
100		for g := 0; g < goroutines; g++ {
101			g := g
102			wg.Add(1)
103			go func() {
104				defer wg.Done()
105				for i := 0; i < iterations; i++ {
106					next := statuses[(g+i)%len(statuses)]
107					// Ignore errors: contention may cause transient failures that are
108					// acceptable (e.g. attempting to set a status that is already set).
109					// The critical invariant is no corruption.
110					_ = s.Update(id, func(bead *Bead) {
111						bead.Status = next
112					})
113				}
114			}()
115		}
116	
117		wg.Wait()
118	
119		// Bead must still exist with a valid status
120		got, err := s.Get(id)
121		require.NoError(t, err, "bead must be readable after concurrent status updates")
122		assert.Contains(t, []string{StatusOpen, StatusInProgress, StatusClosed}, got.Status,
123			"bead must have a valid status after concurrent updates")
124	
125		// JSONL file must be parseable end-to-end
126		allBeads, err := s.ReadAll()
127		require.NoError(t, err, "JSONL file must be parseable after concurrent status transitions")
128		assert.NotEmpty(t, allBeads, "store must not be empty after updates")
129	
130		// Verify no partial writes: every line must decode cleanly
131		for _, bead := range allBeads {
132			assert.NotEmpty(t, bead.ID)
133			assert.NotEmpty(t, bead.Title)
134		}
135	}

137	// TestChaos_ConcurrentCloseAndAppend reproduces the reported bug:
138	// one goroutine closes a bead while another creates a new bead concurrently.
139	// Both operations must persist; neither must clobber the other.
140	func TestChaos_ConcurrentCloseAndAppend(t *testing.T) {
141		t.Parallel()
142		s := newTestBackend(t)
143	
144		// Pre-create the bead that will be closed
145		existing := &Bead{Title: "to-be-closed"}
146		require.NoError(t, s.Create(existing))
147		closeID := existing.ID
148	
149		const rounds = 50
150	
151		for round := 0; round < rounds; round++ {
152			// Re-open the bead so we can close it again
153			require.NoError(t, s.Update(closeID, func(b *Bead) {
154				b.Status = StatusOpen
155			}))
156	
157			newTitle := fmt.Sprintf("new-bead-round-%d", round)
158			var newID string
159	
160			var wg sync.WaitGroup
161			wg.Add(2)
162	
163			// Goroutine A: close the existing bead
164			go func() {
165				defer wg.Done()
166				_ = s.Close(closeID)
167			}()
168	
169			// Goroutine B: create a new bead
170			go func() {
171				defer wg.Done()
172				nb := &Bead{Title: newTitle}
173				if err := s.Create(nb); err == nil {
174					newID = nb.ID
175				}
176			}()
177	
178			wg.Wait()
179	
180			// The new bead must exist
181			if newID != "" {
182				got, err := s.Get(newID)
183				require.NoError(t, err, "round %d: new bead %s must exist", round, newID)
184				assert.Equal(t, newTitle, got.Title, "round %d: new bead title must match", round)
185			}
186	
187			// The closed bead's status must reflect the close (it may have been
188			// re-opened by a subsequent round, but within this round, if the close
189			// completed first and create ran after, the close must be visible).
190			// The critical check: the closed bead must still exist and be parseable.
191			closedBead, err := s.Get(closeID)
192			require.NoError(t, err, "round %d: closed bead must still exist", round)
193			assert.NotEmpty(t, closedBead.ID, "round %d: closed bead ID must not be empty", round)
194		}
195	}

197	// TestChaos_ConcurrentCloseNotLost is the strict regression test for the bug:
198	// close must not be lost when a concurrent create happens after the close acquires
199	// the lock but before it writes. With the directory-lock implementation, the lock
200	// serializes these operations, so the closed status must always be visible after
201	// both goroutines complete (if the close finishes last, the status is closed;
202	// if create finishes last, the store was read AFTER the close, so closed is preserved).
203	func TestChaos_ConcurrentCloseNotLost(t *testing.T) {
204		t.Parallel()
205		s := newTestBackend(t)
206	
207		existing := &Bead{Title: "must-stay-closed"}
208		require.NoError(t, s.Create(existing))
209		closeID := existing.ID
210	
211		const rounds = 20
212	
213		for round := 0; round < rounds; round++ {
214			// Reset to open
215			require.NoError(t, s.Update(closeID, func(b *Bead) {
216				b.Status = StatusOpen
217			}))
218	
219			// Run close and create concurrently, then wait for both
220			var wg sync.WaitGroup
221			wg.Add(2)
222	
223			closeErr := make(chan error, 1)
224			createErr := make(chan error, 1)
225			createdID := make(chan string, 1)
226	
227			go func() {
228				defer wg.Done()
229				closeErr <- s.Close(closeID)
230			}()
231	
232			go func() {
233				defer wg.Done()
234				nb := &Bead{Title: fmt.Sprintf("concurrent-create-%d", round)}
235				err := s.Create(nb)
236				createErr <- err
237				if err == nil {
238					createdID <- nb.ID
239				} else {
240					createdID <- ""
241				}
242			}()
243	
244			wg.Wait()
245	
246			cErr := <-closeErr
247			nErr := <-createErr
248			nID := <-createdID
249	
250			require.NoError(t, cErr, "round %d: close must not error", round)
251			require.NoError(t, nErr, "round %d: create must not error", round)
252	
253			// The closed bead must be closed: since the lock serializes operations,
254			// the create (which reads the file under the lock) must see the post-close state
255			// IF close ran first, OR the close (which reads under the lock) must complete
256			// after create without losing the create. In either ordering, the final file
257			// must reflect both operations.
258			//
259			// The close must be visible: after both complete, whichever ran last wrote
260			// the file. If close ran last → bead is closed. If create ran last → create
261			// read the file AFTER close completed (lock was released), so bead is closed.
262			//
263			// This test documents and verifies the invariant: close is never lost.
264			finalBead, err := s.Get(closeID)
265			require.NoError(t, err, "round %d: closed bead must be readable", round)
266			assert.Equal(t, StatusClosed, finalBead.Status,
267				"round %d: bead must be closed after close+create race — "+
268					"if this fails, the create read a stale snapshot before close wrote", round)
269	
270			// The new bead must also exist
271			if nID != "" {
272				_, err := s.Get(nID)
273				assert.NoError(t, err, "round %d: newly created bead %s must exist", round, nID)
274			}
275		}
276	}

278	// TestChaos_JSONLRoundTripIntegrity writes beads with random/unicode/special
279	// data and verifies all fields survive a round-trip through the JSONL store.
280	func TestChaos_JSONLRoundTripIntegrity(t *testing.T) {
281		t.Parallel()
282	
283		rng := rand.New(rand.NewSource(42))
284	
285		type testCase struct {
286			name  string
287			title string
288			desc  string
289			notes string
290		}
291	
292		cases := []testCase{
293			{name: "ascii", title: "Simple ASCII title", desc: "plain description", notes: ""},
294			{name: "unicode", title: "Unicode: 日本語テスト", desc: "emoji: 🚀🎯✅", notes: "混合 content"},
295			{name: "special-chars", title: `Tabs	and "quotes" and \backslash`, desc: "line1\nline2\nline3", notes: ""},
296			{name: "empty-optional", title: "Only title is required", desc: "", notes: ""},
297			{name: "max-length", title: strings.Repeat("A", 200), desc: strings.Repeat("B", 1000), notes: strings.Repeat("C", 500)},
298			{name: "json-injection", title: `{"injected":"json"}`, desc: `"nested":"value"`, notes: `]},{"id":"fake"`},
299			{name: "null-like", title: "null", desc: "false", notes: "0"},
300			{name: "whitespace", title: "  spaces around  ", desc: "\t\ttabbed\t\t", notes: "   "},
301		}
302	
303		// Add randomized cases
304		for i := 0; i < 10; i++ {
305			cases = append(cases, testCase{
306				name:  fmt.Sprintf("random-%d", i),
307				title: randomString(rng, 5, 80),
308				desc:  randomString(rng, 0, 200),
309				notes: randomString(rng, 0, 100),
310			})
311		}
312	
313		s := newTestBackend(t)
314		created := make([]*Bead, 0, len(cases))
315	
316		for _, tc := range cases {
317			tc := tc
318			b := &Bead{
319				Title:       tc.title,
320				Description: tc.desc,
321				Notes:       tc.notes,
322				Labels:      []string{"chaos", tc.name},
323				Priority:    rng.Intn(5),
324			}
325			if err := s.Create(b); err != nil {
326				// If title is empty after trimming, skip — that's a validation rule, not a bug
327				if strings.Contains(err.Error(), "title is required") {
328					t.Logf("skipping case %q: title trims to empty", tc.name)
329					continue
330				}
331				t.Fatalf("case %q: create failed: %v", tc.name, err)
332			}
333			created = append(created, b)
334		}
335	
336		// Read all back and verify field-by-field
337		beads, err := s.ReadAll()
338		require.NoError(t, err, "ReadAll must succeed after writing random beads")
339	
340		byID := make(map[string]Bead, len(beads))
341		for _, b := range beads {
342			byID[b.ID] = b
343		}
344	
345		for _, orig := range created {
346			got, ok := byID[orig.ID]
347			if !assert.True(t, ok, "bead %s must survive round-trip", orig.ID) {
348				continue
349			}
350			assert.Equal(t, orig.Title, got.Title, "bead %s: Title mismatch", orig.ID)
351			assert.Equal(t, orig.Description, got.Description, "bead %s: Description mismatch", orig.ID)
352			assert.Equal(t, orig.Notes, got.Notes, "bead %s: Notes mismatch", orig.ID)
353			assert.Equal(t, orig.Priority, got.Priority, "bead %s: Priority mismatch", orig.ID)
354			assert.Equal(t, orig.Status, got.Status, "bead %s: Status mismatch", orig.ID)
355			assert.ElementsMatch(t, orig.Labels, got.Labels, "bead %s: Labels mismatch", orig.ID)
356		}
357	}

359	// randomString generates a random string with printable characters including
360	// some unicode, in the range [minLen, maxLen) characters.
361	func randomString(rng *rand.Rand, minLen, maxLen int) string {
362		if maxLen <= minLen {
363			return ""
364		}
365		length := minLen + rng.Intn(maxLen-minLen)
366		if length == 0 {
367			return ""
368		}
369	
370		// Character pools
371		pools := []string{
372			"abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyzABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZ0123456789",
373			` !@#$%^&*()_+-=[]{}|;':\",./<>?`,
374			"αβγδεζηθικλμνξοπρστυφχψω",
375			"日本語中文한국어",
376			"🚀🎯✅❌💡🔧",
377		}
378	
379		var sb strings.Builder
380		for i := 0; i < length; i++ {
381			pool := pools[rng.Intn(len(pools))]
382			runes := []rune(pool)
383			r := runes[rng.Intn(len(runes))]
384			// Skip control characters that would break JSONL lines
385			if r == '\n' || r == '\r' || r == 0 || !unicode.IsPrint(r) {
386				r = 'X'
387			}
388			sb.WriteRune(r)
389		}
390		return sb.String()
391	}

1	package bead
2	
3	import "io"
4	
5	// RawBackend is the low-level storage contract — read/write the entire bead
6	// corpus and serialize concurrent rewrites. JSONLBackend and ExternalBackend
7	// implement it. Higher-level operations (CRUD, claim, ready/blocked, dep ops,
8	// events, archive, JSONL interchange) live on the Backend interface below and
9	// are composed on top of a RawBackend by Store.
10	type RawBackend interface {
11		Init() error
12		ReadAll() ([]Bead, error)
13		WriteAll(beads []Bead) error
14		WithLock(fn func() error) error
15	}
16	
17	// Backend is the high-level bead-tracker contract that callers in
18	// cli/cmd/bead_*.go and the agent loop will eventually program against. It
19	// covers everything the bead description for ddx-bbdd7564 calls out: CRUD,
20	// claim, list/ready/blocked, dep ops, events append, archive split, and
21	// JSONL export/import.
22	//
23	// *Store satisfies this interface; the JSONL implementation lives in store.go
24	// and delegates per-write serialization to the configured RawBackend
25	// (JSONLBackend by default, ExternalBackend when DDX_BEAD_BACKEND or the
26	// .ddx/config.yaml beads.backend field selects bd/br).
27	//
28	// chaos_test.go and other conformance suites should program against this
29	// interface so additional backends can be exercised by the same tests.
30	type Backend interface {
31		// Foundational
32		Init() error
33		ReadAll() ([]Bead, error)
34	
35		// CRUD
36		Create(b *Bead) error
37		Get(id string) (*Bead, error)
38		Update(id string, mutate func(*Bead)) error
39		Close(id string) error
40	
41		// Claim
42		Claim(id, assignee string) error
43		ClaimWithOptions(id, assignee, session, worktree string) error
44		Unclaim(id string) error
45	
46		// Query
47		List(status, label string, where map[string]string) ([]Bead, error)
48		Ready() ([]Bead, error)
49		Blocked() ([]Bead, error)
50	
51		// Dep ops
52		DepAdd(id, depID string) error
53		DepRemove(id, depID string) error
54		DepTree(rootID string) (string, error)
55	
56		// Events
57		AppendEvent(id string, event BeadEvent) error
58		Events(id string) ([]BeadEvent, error)
59	
60		// Archive split
61		Archive(policy ArchivePolicy) ([]string, error)
62		Migrate() (MigrateStats, error)
63	
64		// JSONL interchange
65		Import(source, filePath string) (int, error)
66		ExportTo(w io.Writer) error
67	}
68	
69	// BackendType constants
70	const (
71		BackendJSONL = "jsonl"
72		BackendBD    = "bd"
73		BackendBR    = "br"
74	)
75	
76	const DefaultCollection = "beads"
77	
78	// NewBackend returns a Backend rooted at dir. The selected RawBackend is
79	// resolved from (in priority order): the DDX_BEAD_BACKEND env var, the
80	// .ddx/config.yaml beads.backend field, then the jsonl default. opts are
81	// the same StoreOption values accepted by NewStore.
82	func NewBackend(dir string, opts ...StoreOption) Backend {
83		return NewStore(dir, opts...)
84	}

3	import (

16	// TestChaos_MixedConcurrentOps spawns goroutines each running a different
17	// operation against the same store concurrently: create, claim, close, unclaim,
18	// update, append-event. After all goroutines finish the store must be
19	// parseable, have no duplicate IDs, and all beads must have valid statuses.
20	func TestChaos_MixedConcurrentOps(t *testing.T) {
21		t.Parallel()
22		s := newTestStore(t)
23	
24		// Pre-seed beads that can be operated on.
25		const seedCount = 10
26		seedIDs := make([]string, 0, seedCount)
27		for i := 0; i < seedCount; i++ {
28			b := &Bead{Title: fmt.Sprintf("seed-%d", i)}
29			require.NoError(t, s.Create(b))
30			seedIDs = append(seedIDs, b.ID)
31		}
32	
33		var wg sync.WaitGroup
34		const goroutines = 8
35		const opsPerGoroutine = 20
36	
37		for g := 0; g < goroutines; g++ {
38			g := g
39			wg.Add(1)
40			go func() {
41				defer wg.Done()
42				rng := rand.New(rand.NewSource(int64(g * 1000)))
43	
44				for i := 0; i < opsPerGoroutine; i++ {
45					id := seedIDs[rng.Intn(len(seedIDs))]
46					op := rng.Intn(6)
47					switch op {
48					case 0: // create new
49						nb := &Bead{Title: fmt.Sprintf("g%d-i%d", g, i)}
50						_ = s.Create(nb)
51					case 1: // claim
52						_ = s.Claim(id, fmt.Sprintf("agent-%d", g))
53					case 2: // close
54						_ = s.Close(id)
55					case 3: // unclaim
56						_ = s.Unclaim(id)
57					case 4: // update title
58						_ = s.Update(id, func(b *Bead) {
59							b.Title = fmt.Sprintf("updated-by-g%d-i%d", g, i)
60						})
61					case 5: // append event
62						_ = s.AppendEvent(id, BeadEvent{
63							Kind:    "debug",
64							Summary: fmt.Sprintf("g%d-i%d", g, i),
65						})
66					}
67				}
68			}()
69		}
70	
71		wg.Wait()
72	
73		// Invariants after all concurrent ops.
74		beads, err := s.ReadAll()
75		require.NoError(t, err, "ReadAll must succeed after mixed concurrent ops")
76	
77		validStatuses := []string{StatusOpen, StatusInProgress, StatusClosed}
78	
79		seen := make(map[string]int)
80		for _, b := range beads {

205	// TestChaos_ConcurrentDepManagement hammers DepAdd/DepRemove concurrently
206	// and verifies the dependency graph is always well-formed (no cycles).
207	func TestChaos_ConcurrentDepManagement(t *testing.T) {
208		t.Parallel()
209		s := newTestStore(t)
210	
211		// Create a pool of beads.
212		const poolSize = 6
213		pool := make([]string, 0, poolSize)
214		for i := 0; i < poolSize; i++ {
215			b := &Bead{Title: fmt.Sprintf("dep-pool-%d", i)}
216			require.NoError(t, s.Create(b))
217			pool = append(pool, b.ID)
218		}
219	
220		var wg sync.WaitGroup
221		const goroutines = 4
222		const ops = 30
223	
224		for g := 0; g < goroutines; g++ {
225			g := g
226			wg.Add(1)
227			go func() {
228				defer wg.Done()
229				rng := rand.New(rand.NewSource(int64(g * 7)))
230				for i := 0; i < ops; i++ {
231					// Pick two distinct beads.
232					fromIdx := rng.Intn(poolSize)
233					toIdx := rng.Intn(poolSize)
234					if fromIdx == toIdx {
235						continue
236					}
237					from := pool[fromIdx]
238					to := pool[toIdx]
239					if rng.Intn(2) == 0 {
240						_ = s.DepAdd(from, to) // cycle detection may reject
241					} else {
242						_ = s.DepRemove(from, to) // idempotent
243					}
244				}
245			}()
246		}
247		wg.Wait()
248	
249		// Verify no bead has a self-dependency.
250		beads, err := s.ReadAll()
251		require.NoError(t, err)
252		for _, b := range beads {
253			for _, dep := range b.Dependencies {
254				assert.NotEqual(t, b.ID, dep.DependsOnID,
255					"bead %s must not depend on itself", b.ID)
256			}
257		}
258	}

260	// TestChaos_WriteAllUnderConcurrentReads verifies that WriteAll under
261	// concurrent ReadAll never produces partial/corrupt reads.
262	func TestChaos_WriteAllUnderConcurrentReads(t *testing.T) {
263		t.Parallel()
264		s := newTestStore(t)
265	
266		// Seed initial beads.
267		const seed = 20
268		beads := make([]Bead, 0, seed)
269		for i := 0; i < seed; i++ {
270			b := &Bead{Title: fmt.Sprintf("wa-seed-%d", i)}
271			require.NoError(t, s.Create(b))
272			beads = append(beads, *b)
273		}
274	
275		var wg sync.WaitGroup
276		const writers = 3
277		const readers = 5
278		const iterations = 20
279	
280		// Writers: read-then-write under the store lock (simulates normal update traffic).
281		for w := 0; w < writers; w++ {
282			w := w
283			wg.Add(1)
284			go func() {
285				defer wg.Done()
286				for i := 0; i < iterations; i++ {
287					// Use Update to safely mutate one bead (this uses WithLock internally).
288					id := beads[w%len(beads)].ID
289					_ = s.Update(id, func(b *Bead) {
290						b.Notes = fmt.Sprintf("writer-%d-iter-%d", w, i)
291					})
292				}
293			}()
294		}
295	
296		// Readers: ReadAll must always succeed and return parseable beads with non-empty IDs.
297		errCh := make(chan error, readers*iterations)
298		for r := 0; r < readers; r++ {
299			wg.Add(1)
300			go func() {
301				defer wg.Done()
302				for i := 0; i < iterations; i++ {
303					got, err := s.ReadAll()
304					if err != nil {
305						errCh <- err
306						continue
307					}
308					for _, b := range got {
309						if b.ID == "" {
310							errCh <- fmt.Errorf("empty ID in concurrent read")
311						}
312					}
313				}
314			}()
315		}
316	
317		wg.Wait()
318		close(errCh)
319	
320		var errs []error
321		for err := range errCh {
322			errs = append(errs, err)
323		}
324		assert.Empty(t, errs, "concurrent ReadAll must not see corrupt state: %v", errs)
325	}

532	// TestChaos_DuplicateRowFolding directly tests foldLatestBeads with handcrafted
533	// duplicate scenarios to verify it always keeps the last written row.

704						// Also increment a custom counter in Extra.
705						if b.Extra == nil {
706							b.Extra = make(map[string]any)
707						}
708					})
709				}
710			}()
711		}
712		wg.Wait()
713	
714		// Extra must still have the original fields.
715		got, err := s.Get(id)
716		require.NoError(t, err)
717		require.NotNil(t, got.Extra)
718	
719		tag, ok := got.Extra["custom-tag"]
720		assert.True(t, ok, "custom-tag must survive concurrent updates")
721		assert.Equal(t, "important", tag)
722	
723		specID, ok := got.Extra["spec-id"]
724		assert.True(t, ok, "spec-id must survive concurrent updates")
725		assert.Equal(t, "FEAT-999", specID)
726	}

733	// assertValidJSONLFile reads the raw JSONL file and verifies every non-empty
734	// line is valid JSON. Used as a hard assertion that the file is never corrupt.
735	func assertValidJSONLFile(t *testing.T, s *Store) {
736		t.Helper()
737		data, err := os.ReadFile(s.File)
738		require.NoError(t, err, "JSONL file must be readable")
739	
740		lines := strings.Split(string(data), "\n")
741		for i, line := range lines {
742			line = strings.TrimSpace(line)
743			if line == "" {
744				continue
745			}
746			// Must be parseable JSON.
747			b, parseErr := unmarshalBead([]byte(line))
748			assert.NoError(t, parseErr, "line %d must be valid JSON: %q", i+1, line)
749			if parseErr == nil {
750				assert.NotEmpty(t, b.ID, "line %d must have non-empty ID", i+1)
751			}
752		}
753	}

755	// TestChaos_JSOLFileAlwaysValidJSON verifies that after any sequence of ops
756	// the raw JSONL file contains only valid JSON lines.
757	func TestChaos_JSONLFileAlwaysValidJSON(t *testing.T) {
758		t.Parallel()
759		s := newTestStore(t)
760	
761		rng := rand.New(rand.NewSource(55555))
762		var ids []string
763	
764		// Build up state.
765		for i := 0; i < 10; i++ {
766			b := &Bead{Title: fmt.Sprintf("valid-json-bead-%d", i)}
767			require.NoError(t, s.Create(b))
768			ids = append(ids, b.ID)
769		}
770		assertValidJSONLFile(t, s)
771	
772		ops := []func(){
773			func() { _ = s.Close(ids[rng.Intn(len(ids))]) },
774			func() { _ = s.Claim(ids[rng.Intn(len(ids))], "agent") },
775			func() { _ = s.Unclaim(ids[rng.Intn(len(ids))]) },
776			func() {
777				nb := &Bead{Title: "new-bead"}
778				if err := s.Create(nb); err == nil {
779					ids = append(ids, nb.ID)
780				}
781			},
782			func() {
783				_ = s.AppendEvent(ids[rng.Intn(len(ids))], BeadEvent{
784					Kind:    "evidence",
785					Summary: "check",
786				})
787			},
788		}
789	
790		for i := 0; i < 50; i++ {
791			ops[rng.Intn(len(ops))]()
792			assertValidJSONLFile(t, s)
793		}
794	}

102	// TestExternalBackendOpensBeadsArchiveWithFallback verifies that opening the
103	// "beads-archive" collection through the bd external backend produces a
104	// working read/write path via the JSONL fallback, without invoking bd.
105	// bd/br do not yet expose per-collection scoping in their CLI surface, so
106	// non-default collections must round-trip through .ddx/<collection>.jsonl
107	// to avoid colliding with the tool's primary store.
108	func TestExternalBackendOpensBeadsArchiveWithFallback(t *testing.T) {
109		toolDir := t.TempDir()
110		// A fake bd that always errors. If the backend ever shells out for the
111		// archive collection, ReadAll/WriteAll will fail and this test will
112		// catch the regression.
113		failScript := "#!/bin/sh\necho 'fake bd should not be called for non-default collections' 1>&2\nexit 1\n"
114		failBd := filepath.Join(toolDir, "bd")
115		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(failBd, []byte(failScript), 0o755))
116		t.Setenv("PATH", toolDir+string(os.PathListSeparator)+os.Getenv("PATH"))
117		t.Setenv("DDX_BEAD_BACKEND", "bd")
118	
119		dir := t.TempDir()
120		ddxDir := filepath.Join(dir, ".ddx")
121		s := NewStore(ddxDir, WithCollection(BeadsArchiveCollection))
122	
123		backend, ok := s.backend.(*ExternalBackend)
124		require.True(t, ok, "expected ExternalBackend for bd + non-default collection")
125		require.Equal(t, "bd", backend.Tool)
126		require.Equal(t, BeadsArchiveCollection, backend.Collection)
127		require.NotNil(t, backend.fallback, "non-default collection must have JSONL fallback")
128	
129		// Init must not panic and must create the archive file under .ddx/.
130		require.NoError(t, s.Init())
131		archivePath := filepath.Join(ddxDir, BeadsArchiveCollection+".jsonl")
132		_, err := os.Stat(archivePath)
133		require.NoError(t, err, "archive file should exist after Init")
134	
135		// Empty read works.
136		got, err := s.ReadAll()
137		require.NoError(t, err)
138		assert.Empty(t, got)
139	
140		// Round-trip a bead through WriteAll/ReadAll without touching bd.
141		want := Bead{ID: "ddx-arch-1", Title: "archived item", Status: StatusClosed, IssueType: "task", Priority: 2}
142		require.NoError(t, s.WriteAll([]Bead{want}))
143		got, err = s.ReadAll()
144		require.NoError(t, err)
145		require.Len(t, got, 1)
146		assert.Equal(t, want.ID, got[0].ID)
147		assert.Equal(t, want.Title, got[0].Title)
148	}

580	func TestHeartbeatReclaimStaleInProgressBead(t *testing.T) {
581		withHeartbeat(t, 10*time.Millisecond, 50*time.Millisecond)
582	
583		s := newTestStore(t)
584		b := &Bead{ID: "ddx-hb-stale", Title: "Stale claim"}
585		require.NoError(t, s.Create(b))
586	
587		// First worker claims it normally.
588		require.NoError(t, s.Claim(b.ID, "worker-a"))
589	
590		// Forge a stale heartbeat by rewriting the store under its lock.
591		require.NoError(t, s.Update(b.ID, func(bd *Bead) {
592			if bd.Extra == nil {
593				bd.Extra = map[string]any{}
594			}
595			stale := time.Now().UTC().Add(-1 * time.Hour).Format(time.RFC3339)
596			bd.Extra["execute-loop-heartbeat-at"] = stale
597			bd.Extra["claimed-at"] = stale
598		}))
599	
600		// A fresh worker must be able to reclaim the stalled bead.
601		require.NoError(t, s.Claim(b.ID, "worker-b"))
602		got, err := s.Get(b.ID)
603		require.NoError(t, err)
604		assert.Equal(t, StatusInProgress, got.Status)
605		assert.Equal(t, "worker-b", got.Owner)
606	
607		// The ready-execution queue must surface the stale bead too.
608		s2 := newTestStore(t)
609		orig := &Bead{ID: "ddx-hb-stale-2", Title: "Stale claim 2"}
610		require.NoError(t, s2.Create(orig))
611		require.NoError(t, s2.Claim(orig.ID, "worker-a"))
612		require.NoError(t, s2.Update(orig.ID, func(bd *Bead) {
613			stale := time.Now().UTC().Add(-1 * time.Hour).Format(time.RFC3339)
614			bd.Extra["execute-loop-heartbeat-at"] = stale
615		}))
616		ready, err := s2.ReadyExecution()
617		require.NoError(t, err)
618		require.Len(t, ready, 1)
619		assert.Equal(t, orig.ID, ready[0].ID)
620	}

655		s := newTestStore(t)

657		require.NoError(t, s.Create(b))

662		start := make(chan struct{})
663		wg.Add(n)

683	// TestConcurrentUpdates_DifferentBeads spawns N=16 goroutines each updating a
684	// distinct bead. All updates must land and the resulting JSONL must be valid
685	// (no interleaved lines, no truncation, no lost updates).
686	func TestConcurrentUpdates_DifferentBeads(t *testing.T) {
687		const n = 16
688		s := newTestStore(t)
689	
690		// Pre-create one bead per goroutine.
691		ids := make([]string, n)
692		for i := 0; i < n; i++ {
693			b := &Bead{Title: fmt.Sprintf("bead-%d", i)}
694			require.NoError(t, s.Create(b))
695			ids[i] = b.ID
696		}
697	
698		var wg sync.WaitGroup
699		wg.Add(n)
700		for i := 0; i < n; i++ {
701			i := i
702			go func() {
703				defer wg.Done()
704				err := s.Update(ids[i], func(b *Bead) {
705					b.Notes = fmt.Sprintf("updated-by-goroutine-%d", i)
706				})
707				assert.NoError(t, err, "goroutine %d update must not error", i)
708			}()
709		}
710		wg.Wait()
711	
712		// All beads must have received their update.
713		for i := 0; i < n; i++ {
714			got, err := s.Get(ids[i])
715			require.NoError(t, err, "bead %s must be readable after concurrent updates", ids[i])
716			assert.Equal(t, fmt.Sprintf("updated-by-goroutine-%d", i), got.Notes,
717				"bead %d must carry its update", i)
718		}
719	
720		// JSONL file must be fully parseable with no truncated/interleaved lines.
721		data, err := os.ReadFile(s.File)
722		require.NoError(t, err)
723		scanner := bufio.NewScanner(strings.NewReader(string(data)))
724		var lineCount int
725		for scanner.Scan() {
726			line := strings.TrimSpace(scanner.Text())
727			if line == "" {
728				continue
729			}
730			lineCount++
731			// Each line must be valid JSON (starts with '{' and ends with '}').
732			assert.True(t, strings.HasPrefix(line, "{") && strings.HasSuffix(line, "}"),
733				"JSONL line must be a complete JSON object: %q", line)
734		}
735		require.NoError(t, scanner.Err())
736		assert.Equal(t, n, lineCount, "JSONL must contain exactly %d lines", n)
737	}

739	// TestPartialWriteCleanup verifies that stale tmp files left by a crashed
740	// writer are cleaned up when a new write completes, and the real file is
741	// unaffected by their presence.
742	func TestPartialWriteCleanup(t *testing.T) {
743		s := newTestStore(t)
744	
745		b := &Bead{Title: "survivor"}
746		require.NoError(t, s.Create(b))
747	
748		// Simulate a crashed writer: drop a stale .tmp-* file in the same dir.
749		staleContent := []byte(`{"id":"ghost","title":"ghost","type":"task","status":"open","priority":0,"created":"2026-01-01T00:00:00Z","updated":"2026-01-01T00:00:00Z"}` + "\n")
750		staleTmp := s.File + ".tmp-99999-deadbeef"
751		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(staleTmp, staleContent, 0o644))
752	
753		// Perform a normal update — should succeed regardless of the stale tmp file.
754		require.NoError(t, s.Update(b.ID, func(b *Bead) {
755			b.Notes = "after stale tmp"
756		}))
757	
758		// The stale tmp file is not automatically removed by the store (it's left
759		// for the OS/operator), but the real file must be correct and not contain
760		// the ghost bead.
761		beads, err := s.ReadAll()
762		require.NoError(t, err)
763		require.Len(t, beads, 1, "real file must contain exactly 1 bead")
764		assert.Equal(t, b.ID, beads[0].ID)
765		assert.Equal(t, "after stale tmp", beads[0].Notes)
766	
767		// The stale tmp file itself must not have been renamed over the real file.
768		for _, bead := range beads {
769			assert.NotEqual(t, "ghost", bead.ID, "ghost bead from stale tmp must not appear")
770		}
771	}

225		// same ddx_beads collection; the events stay accessible via Events()
226		// (the Store externalises them to the attachment sidecar on close).
227		events, err := s.Events(b.ID)
228		require.NoError(t, err)
229		assert.Len(t, events, 1)
230	}
231	
232	// TestAxonBackend_JSONLImportExportRoundTrip verifies the bd/br interchange
233	// contract: a bead exported through the axon-backed store and re-imported
234	// re-creates the same bead corpus. This is the AC §"JSONL export/import for
235	// bd/br round-trip" knot.
236	func TestAxonBackend_JSONLImportExportRoundTrip(t *testing.T) {
237		t.Parallel()
238		src := newAxonStore(t)
239	
240		// Seed a small corpus with deps + events.
241		a := &Bead{Title: "alpha"}
242		bb := &Bead{Title: "beta"}
243		require.NoError(t, src.Create(a))
244		require.NoError(t, src.Create(bb))
245		require.NoError(t, src.DepAdd(bb.ID, a.ID))
246		require.NoError(t, src.AppendEvent(a.ID, BeadEvent{Kind: "k", Summary: "s"}))
247	
248		var buf bytes.Buffer
249		require.NoError(t, src.ExportTo(&buf))
250		require.NotZero(t, buf.Len())
251	
252		// Write the export to a temp file and import into a fresh axon-backed
253		// store. The expected count should equal the source corpus.
254		jsonlPath := filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), "exported.jsonl")
255		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(jsonlPath, buf.Bytes(), 0o644))
256	
257		dst := newAxonStore(t)
258		count, err := dst.Import("jsonl", jsonlPath)
259		require.NoError(t, err)
260		assert.Equal(t, 2, count)
261	
262		gotA, err := dst.Get(a.ID)
263		require.NoError(t, err)
264		assert.Equal(t, "alpha", gotA.Title)
265	
266		gotB, err := dst.Get(bb.ID)
267		require.NoError(t, err)
268		assert.Equal(t, "beta", gotB.Title)
269		require.Len(t, gotB.Dependencies, 1)
270		assert.Equal(t, a.ID, gotB.Dependencies[0].DependsOnID)
271	}
272	
273	// TestAxonBackend_AtomicWriteSnapshotIntact proves WriteAll uses temp+rename
274	// (writeAtomicFile) and does not leak partial state between writes.
275	func TestAxonBackend_AtomicWriteSnapshotIntact(t *testing.T) {
276		t.Parallel()
277		s := newAxonStore(t)
278	
279		for i := 0; i < 5; i++ {
280			require.NoError(t, s.Create(&Bead{Title: fmt.Sprintf("b-%d", i)}))
281		}
282	
283		ax := s.backend.(*AxonBackend)
284		// No temp files should be visible after a quiescent WriteAll cycle.
285		entries, err := os.ReadDir(ax.Dir)
286		require.NoError(t, err)
287		for _, e := range entries {
288			assert.False(t, strings.HasPrefix(e.Name(), AxonBeadsCollection+".jsonl.tmp-"),
289				"unexpected temp file %s", e.Name())
290			assert.False(t, strings.HasPrefix(e.Name(), AxonEventsCollection+".jsonl.tmp-"),
291				"unexpected temp file %s", e.Name())
292		}
293	
294		all, err := s.List("", "", nil)
295		require.NoError(t, err)
296		assert.Len(t, all, 5)
297	}
298	
299	// TestAxonBackend_ConcurrentClaimsSerialised reproduces the chaos contract
300	// that two claimers must not both succeed: WithLock + writeAtomicFile must
301	// keep the read-modify-write race correct under the axon backend.
302	func TestAxonBackend_ConcurrentClaimsSerialised(t *testing.T) {
303		t.Parallel()
304		s := newAxonStore(t)
305	
306		b := &Bead{Title: "race"}
307		require.NoError(t, s.Create(b))
308	
309		const goroutines = 8
310		var (
311			wg       sync.WaitGroup
312			successN int
313			mu       sync.Mutex
314		)
315		for i := 0; i < goroutines; i++ {
316			wg.Add(1)
317			go func(i int) {
318				defer wg.Done()
319				err := s.Claim(b.ID, fmt.Sprintf("worker-%d", i))
320				if err == nil {
321					mu.Lock()
322					successN++
323					mu.Unlock()
324				}
325			}(i)
326		}
327		wg.Wait()
328		assert.Equal(t, 1, successN, "exactly one claim must win the race")
329	
330		got, err := s.Get(b.ID)
331		require.NoError(t, err)
332		assert.Equal(t, StatusInProgress, got.Status)
333	}
334	
335	// TestAxonBackend_SelectableViaConfigKnobWithFlag proves AC §3: the backend
336	// is selected by beads.backend=axon (via DDX_BEAD_BACKEND) only when the
337	// experimental flag is set. Without the flag, beads.backend=axon must fall
338	// through to the JSONL default and not silently corrupt the workspace.
339	func TestAxonBackend_SelectableViaConfigKnobWithFlag(t *testing.T) {
340		t.Setenv("DDX_BEAD_BACKEND", BackendAxon)
341		t.Setenv(AxonExperimentalEnv, "1")
342	
343		dir := filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), ".ddx")
344		s := NewStore(dir)
345		_, ok := s.backend.(*AxonBackend)
346		assert.True(t, ok, "DDX_BEAD_BACKEND=axon plus the experimental flag must select AxonBackend")
347	}
348	
349	func TestAxonBackend_ConfigKnobIgnoredWithoutFlag(t *testing.T) {
350		t.Setenv("DDX_BEAD_BACKEND", BackendAxon)
351		t.Setenv(AxonExperimentalEnv, "")
352	
353		dir := filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), ".ddx")
354		s := NewStore(dir)
355		assert.Nil(t, s.backend, "without the feature flag, beads.backend=axon must fall through to the built-in JSONL path")
356	}
357	
358	func TestAxonExperimentalEnabledTruthyValues(t *testing.T) {
359		for _, val := range []string{"1", "true", "TRUE", "Yes", "on"} {
360			t.Run(val, func(t *testing.T) {
361				t.Setenv(AxonExperimentalEnv, val)
362				assert.True(t, AxonExperimentalEnabled(), "%q should enable the flag", val)
363			})
364		}
365		for _, val := range []string{"", "0", "false", "no", "off", "garbage"} {
366			t.Run("disabled-"+val, func(t *testing.T) {
367				t.Setenv(AxonExperimentalEnv, val)
368				assert.False(t, AxonExperimentalEnabled(), "%q should not enable the flag", val)
369			})
370		}
371	}
372	
373	// TestAxonBackend_OrphanEventsDropped verifies that an event entity whose
374	// event_of points at a non-existent bead is silently dropped on read. This
375	// is the partial-write recovery story documented in WriteAll.
376	func TestAxonBackend_OrphanEventsDropped(t *testing.T) {
377		t.Parallel()
378		s := newAxonStore(t)
379	
380		b := &Bead{Title: "live"}
381		require.NoError(t, s.Create(b))
382		require.NoError(t, s.AppendEvent(b.ID, BeadEvent{Kind: "k", Summary: "live-event"}))
383	
384		// Manually append an orphan event for a bead that does not exist.
385		ax := s.backend.(*AxonBackend)
386		orphan := axonEventEnvelope{
387			Collection:    AxonEventsCollection,
388			SchemaVersion: axonSchemaVersion,
389			EventOf:       "ddx-deadbeef",
390			Index:         0,
391			Event:         BeadEvent{Kind: "orphan", Summary: "orphaned", CreatedAt: time.Now().UTC()},
392		}
393		row, err := json.Marshal(orphan)
394		require.NoError(t, err)
395		f, err := os.OpenFile(ax.EventsFile, os.O_APPEND|os.O_WRONLY, 0o644)
396		require.NoError(t, err)
397		_, err = f.Write(append(row, '\n'))
398		require.NoError(t, err)
399		require.NoError(t, f.Close())
400	
401		// Live bead's events still readable; orphan does not surface.
402		events, err := s.Events(b.ID)
403		require.NoError(t, err)
404		require.Len(t, events, 1)
405		assert.Equal(t, "live-event", events[0].Summary)
406	
407		// And there is no phantom bead with the orphan id.
408		_, err = s.Get("ddx-deadbeef")
409		assert.Error(t, err)
410	}

413		out := make(map[string]bool, len(beads))
414		for _, b := range beads {
415			out[b.ID] = true
416		}
417		return out
418	}
